rows.signal: legacy chat-read route returned a chat whose content carried the victim tenant's planted canary when the owner-id field pointed at a principal in another tenant
rows.why: a caller in tenant A read tenant B's decrypted chat by setting a client-supplied owner id the route never authorized.

controls.negative: no-owner-id request and owner-id=self both returned 404 / no cross-tenant data
controls.positive: same owner id against the validating sibling route returned 403 — proves the guard exists and the legacy route lacks it
controls.canary: benign distinctive marker planted in the victim chat (literal value scrubbed)
controls.replay: reproduced in a fresh session; cross-tenant artifact operator-confirmed

rows.signal: an ordinary seat got an answer from a model outside the company allowlist, with response metadata showing that model actually answered
rows.why: the company allowlist was only loaded for a privileged user class, so for an ordinary seat the server-side check ran against an absent allowlist and failed open.

controls.positive: the unconditional model filter (held defense) coerced its target value — confirms server-side coercion is possible here, isolating the allowlist load-gating as the bug
controls.canary: n/a (capability-execution finding, not a token leak)
controls.replay: reproduced fresh session; asserted served model != requested-allowed model

rows.signal: a tool an admin had deactivated emitted tool events after being forced via the client mandatory-tools list
rows.why: the client-supplied mandatory-tools list outranked the server's deactivated-tools policy, dispatching a disabled capability.

controls.negative: normal request (no mandatory-tools) → the deactivated tool was absent
controls.positive: a sibling family (web search) was correctly removed at toolset assembly — the held counterpart showing the right place to enforce
controls.canary: n/a (tool actually fired its events, not merely listed)

rows.signal: a script-shaped payload in model output was reflected into the diagram/markdown renderer's output
rows.why: reflection is not execution — the renderer sandboxed/escaped the payload and no execution canary fired, so there is no XSS.

controls.positive: execution detector validated against a known-executing case before trusting the negative
controls.canary: inert benign execution marker (would have fired if it ran) — did not fire
